summary: Gruber describes his frustration with toggling closed captions on Apple TV while watching The Gentlemen on Netflix, where cockney accents required frequent caption use. He discovers two better methods than the on-screen toggle: the iPhone Control Center remote's CC button and the tvOS accessibility shortcut via triple-clicking the Menu/Back button. However, Netflix's tvOS app supports neither method, only its own custom player toggle. Gruber argues Apple should mandate accessibility feature support through App Store review, and notes that testing across a dozen popular tvOS apps shows almost none support the accessibility shortcut either.
key_points: tvOS offers a triple-click accessibility shortcut and iPhone Control Center CC button for toggling captions, but most streaming apps ignore these system-level features
Netflix only supports its own custom on-screen caption toggle, bypassing Apple's accessibility infrastructure
Siri voice commands for captions work across most streaming apps including Netflix, but this doesn't excuse the lack of shortcut support
Apple should mandate support for accessibility features like caption toggling through App Store review
Testing across a dozen popular tvOS apps found only Paramount+ and ESPN support the accessibility shortcut
money_quotes: I find captions highly distracting if I don't need them.
I tried just leaving captions on, but I really do find it unbearably distracting when I do understand what's being said.
I get why Netflix and other streaming apps want to use their own custom video players, but it ought to be mandated by App Store review that they support accessibility features like this one.

All cool features which seem to work in all major tvOS streaming apps, including Netflix — but no excuse not to support the triple-click accessibility shortcut and the CC button in the Control Center remote.
Only works in Paramount+ and ESPN. Just terrible.
key_insight: Apple should use App Store review to mandate that streaming apps support system-level accessibility features like the caption toggle shortcut, rather than allowing apps to only implement their own custom controls.
